business.headline: Government waives customs duty on key petrochemical imports amid West Asia warThe Finance Ministry has reduced duty to nil on 40 petrochemical products, with the exemption coming into effect from April 2 and remaining in force till June 30, 2026.

Sensex crashes after Trump's speech on Iran war; Rs 10 lakh crore wiped outThe stock market saw a sharp sell-off, with the Sensex falling over 1,500 points and the Nifty dropping over2%, slipping below the 22,200 mark.

Markets sink as Sensex drops 1,300 points after Trump's speech on Iran warMarkets fell after US President Donald Trump's remarks on a possible escalation in the Iran conflict, which hit investor sentiment. He warned of strong military action, including potential strikes on oil infrastructure, even as he said diplomatic talks are still ongoing.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market crash after Trump's fresh speech?Early indicators suggest a soft start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 22,359 at 7:41 am, pointing to a gap-down opening for the Nifty 50, which had closed at 22,679.40 in the previous session.

PPF vs SIP: Where will Rs 50,000 to Rs 1 lakh grow faster in 10 years?You're putting aside Rs 50,000 to Rs 1 lakh every month, aiming to build a solid corpus over the next 10 years. But the big question remains, i.e., should you play it safe with PPF, or chase higher growth with SIPs?

Iran war hits Indian bedrooms: How the conflict triggered a condom shortageAs the geopolitical tremors of the Iran war ripple through global supply chains, an intimate but essential public health commodity has become the latest casualty.

DA hike: What's causing the delay and how much can employees expect?The delay in the DA hike announcement has caught attention this year, especially among employees and pensioners who track it closely. But is this unusual, and more importantly, what kind of increase is likely this time?
Does your credit score matter when applying for a bank job?You may think your credit score only matters for loans, but could it also affect your chances of getting a bank job? Let's find out.

Sensex rallies over 1,100 points in a strong start to FY27The BSE Sensex rose 1,186.77 points, or 1.65%, to close at 73,134.32. The Nifty 50 gained 348 points, or 1.56%, to settle at 22,679.40, holding firmly above the 22,600 mark.

Blinkit brings quick commerce inside Mumbai Airport terminalsThe service allows travellers to order essentials directly through the Blinkit app and have them delivered anywhere within the departure area, including boarding gates, lounges, food courts, and select partner outlets.

Brent crude oil slips below $99 as Trump hints at possible end to conflictThe effective closure of the Strait has already started to impact global markets. Reduced supply has pushed energy prices higher and raised fears of inflation.

Is India's LPG crisis over or does a new threat loom amid Iran war?There are early signs of possible de-escalation in the West Asia war, but experts say that the current situation should be seen as 'temporary relief, not a full all-clear', even as supply conditions have improved.

Paying over Rs 50,000 rent? Don't ignore this 2% TDS ruleIf your rent crosses Rs 50,000 a month, there's more to do than just paying your landlord on time. A key TDS rule applies, and missing it could lead to extra costs.

From LPG to ATM charges: 7 changes hitting your everyday finances from todayYour daily expenses may not stay the same this year. Starting today, changes in LPG prices, ATM usage rules and more are set to impact how you manage your money.

Jet fuel price hiked: Will your flight tickets cost more?With ATF prices rising and global oil markets volatile, how will the hike impact domestic and international flight travel in the days ahead? Read on to find out.

From HRA to gift vouchers: 9 big income tax changes affecting you from April 1A new financial year often brings fresh rules, and this time is no different. Starting April 1, multiple income tax changes, from allowances to workplace perks, are set to influence your day-to-day finances. Let's have a look.

Garden Reach shares jump 18%: Why GRSE stock price rose today?GRSE's strong financial results boost investor confidence and lift shipbuilding stocks. GRSE reported a record annual turnover of Rs 6,400 crore for FY26, compared to Rs 5,076 crore in the previous financial year, marking a 26% rise.

Oracle's layoffs explained: Why the IT giant suddenly fired 30,000 employeesIndia appears to be among the worst-hit markets, with reports indicating that nearly 12,000 employees may have been laid off out of a workforce of around 30,000.

IndiGo shares jump 9%: Why are aviation stocks rising today?IndiGo shares climbed as much as 8.33% to Rs 4272.20, while SpiceJet rose over 4.4% to Rs 10.17 around 9:48 am, signalling continued buying interest in the sector.

entertainment.headline: Alia Bhatt calls Ramayana glimpse out of this world, praises Ranbir Kapoor Alia Bhatt has shared her first reaction to Ramayana's Rama glimpse, praising Ranbir Kapoor. Here's what she revealed ahead of its release.

Ramayana teaser unveils Ranbir Kapoor as Rama: Grand vision of duty, dharma, destiny Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana unveils its first look with Ranbir Kapoor as Rama, promising a visually grand and emotionally layered retelling. The epic explores choices, duty, and sacrifice through a fresh cinematic lens.

Hera Pheri 3 in limbo: Writer exits as rights battle derails Priyadarshan's film Hera Pheri 3 faces fresh uncertainty as writer Rohan Shankar steps away amid ongoing legal disputes over rights. The film's future remains unclear following court battles that continue to stall production.

Youth, grit and a tea shop deal: The Ken Karunaas story Tamil cinema needed Ken Karunaas, son of actor-politician Karunas, has emerged as a promising actor-director in Tamil cinema. The 24-year-old actor's debut film as director, Youth, has garnered critical and commercial success, marking a new chapter in his career.

Why not India? Ranbir Kapoor's fans question Ramayana teaser launch in LA Ranbir Kapoor's Ramayana teaser has triggered a debate after its LA-first release. Here's why fans are upset and what it means for the film.

Kathanar: Is Anushka Shetty's Nila actually Kalyani Priyadarshan's Neeli from Lokah? The trailer of Kathanar has sparked debate over Anushka Shetty's limited presence. The theory is also gaining ground that Anushka's Nila could be Kalyani Priyadarshan's Neeli from Lokah, and that the two characters are based on the same folklore.

5 reasons why Dhurandhar 2 dethroning Dangal sounds good for Indian cinema As Dhurandhar 2 eyes Dangal's long-standing box office record as the highest-grossing film in India, what would a new-era blockbuster topping the charts mean for Indian cinema's growth?

BTS  2.0 music video out. Swagger, symbolism, and a confident Bangtan BTS return with 2.0, a slick, defiant music video inspired by Oldboy, blending striking visuals, sharp choreography, and signature Bangtan confidence.

Megan Thee Stallion calls mid-show health scare a 'wake-up call': It scared me Rapper Megan Thee Stallion was hospitalised after a mid-show health scare during Moulin Rouge! Find out what happened and when she plans to return.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 14: Ranveer film races towards Rs 1500 crore globally Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge has grossed over Rs 1,435 crore globally in less than a fortnight. In the domestic market, the film has surpassed Rs 900 crore so far.

Rahul Arunoday Banerjee death: No doctor at clinic, says production house The production company, Moments Motion Pictures, issued an official statement over the death of Bengali actor Rahul Arunoday Banerjee. The statement claimed that those present at the site had attempted to save the actor, whose death has raised concerns over actors' safety on the sets.

Dada: Rajkummar Rao begins filming Sourav Ganguly biopic at Eden Gardens. Pics Rajkummar Rao has officially begun the shooting for his sports-biopic, titled Dada: The Sourav Ganguly Story. The actor shared the first pictures from the set on social media.

Filmmaker Soumik Sen on why he cast Bangladeshi actor Arifin Shuvoo in Jazz City Filmmaker Soumik Sen discusses Jazz City, his exploration of the Bangladesh Liberation War, cultural identity, and the overlooked history of the eastern front, while explaining his unconventional casting choices.

Cocktail 2: Shahid, Kriti and Rashmika's song Jab Talak out on April 8 The new posters of Homi Adajania's Cocktail 2 were unveiled on April 1 along with the date announcement for the song Jab Talak. The track featuring Shahid Kapoor, Kriti Sanon and Rashmika Mandanna will be released on April 8.

Vedang Raina calls working with Imtiaz Ali a once-in-a-lifetime experience Actor Vedang Raina opened up about feeling "spoiled" after working with Imtiaz Ali on Main Vaapas Aaunga. Here's what he revealed about the experience.

Mammootty, Mohanlal's Patriot new release date announced. Details inside The Malayalam film Patriot, starring Mammootty and Mohanlal, has been postponed and will now release on a fresh date. The makers assured fans that the delay was due to unforeseen reasons and promised a high-quality cinematic experience.

Megan Thee Stallion hospitalised after mid-show exit during Broadway performance Rapper and singer Megan Thee Stallion was rushed to hospital after falling ill during a Broadway performance of Moulin Rouge! The Musical in New York. Due to the health scare, she was forced to make an abrupt exit before the show had concluded.

The Pitt's Laetitia Hollard on what went behind the sensitive rape kit episode The Pitt Season 2 featured a deeply sensitive episode on sexual assault. Actor Laetitia Hollard highlighted the extensive preparation and empathy involved in portraying trauma-informed care.

Zendaya plans to disappear after packed 2026 slate, fears fans may get sick of her Hollywood star Zendaya is set to dominate 2026 with a series of thrilling projects. After this bustling year, she plans to take a brief break from the limelight, promising a return in 2027.

It's all about oilAs Iran solidifies its grip on the Strait of Hormuz, the crucial oil corridor, businessman Trump seems to have smelled an opportunity - buy oil and gas from America. She described a surge of 'depressed' troops spending heavily and casually revealing they were heading to the Middle East soon.

28 astronauts went to the Moon. But how many actually set foot on it?Including the four on Artemis 2, 28 astronauts have been on lunar missions. But how many humans have actually set foot on the Moon? This story will tell you this and about those who flew close but didn't land.

MS Dhoni comeback earlier than 2 weeks? CSK star back training at Chennai IPL 2026: MS Dhoni's viral net session, where he smashed sixes, has sparked hopes of an early CSK return after a calf strain, with the five-time champions eager for his experience.

Australian journeyman Ryan Williams soaks in fairytale India debut goalRyan Williams said his dream India debut felt surreal after scoring early in a 2-1 win over Hong Kong, calling the moment "blurry" as he helped end a tough qualifying campaign on a high.

Indian Olympic Association forms ad-hoc panel to run Equestrian Federation of IndiaThe IOA has formed a four-member ad-hoc panel to run the Equestrian Federation of India, following a Delhi High Court directive, with a mandate to reform governance and conduct fresh elections.

IPL 2026: LSG hold back Mayank Yadav return, no Delhi debut for Auqib NabiMayank Yadav and Auqib Nabi have been made to wait for their chance in the IPL 2026 as LSG and DC decided to hold back their pacers for the clash at the Ekana stadium on Wednesday, April 1.

Verstappen retirement talk, Bearmann crash lead F1's review into new regulationsFollowing Oliver Bearman's crash at the Japanese Grand Prix, Formula 1's governing body, the FIA, has announced a series of meetings in April to review the new 2026 regulations. With races in Bahrain and Saudi Arabia called off, the governing body will use the unexpected break to reassess the rules. The decision also comes amid growing pressure after Max Verstappen hinted at a possible retirement.

LSG vs DC Highlights: Rizvi guides Delhi Capitals to an emphatic 6-wicket winLucknow (LSG) vs Delhi (DC) Highlights, IPL 2026: Sameer Rizvi finishes in style with a six as Delhi Capitals seal a six-wicket win over LSG, completing a composed chase after early pressure and capping a match-winning knock.

India to play T20I series in Zimbabwe 4 days after finishing England tourThe Indian Cricket Board has announced a 3-match tour of Zimbabwe in the month of July. The Indian team will be playing in Africa, four days after completing their tour of England.

In Lucknow, Rishabh Pant searches for his perfect Galauti balanceRishabh Pant begins IPL 2026 in Lucknow leaner and more focused. He is seeking a Galauti-like balance between flair and control as he looks to rediscover consistency and reclaim his place in India's white-ball plans.

FIFA refuses to shift Iran's World Cup matches despite Donald Trump's threatFIFA has refused to shift Iran's World Cup matches out of the United States. Gianni Infantino has stood his ground that the schedule will remain unchanged despite Donald Trump hinting at threats to Iranian players.

India Women's record broken as Amelia Kerr leads New Zealand's historic ODI chaseNZW vs SAW, 2nd ODI: Amelia Kerr scored her fifth ODI century as New Zealand beat South Africa by two wickets in Wellington on Wednesday, completing the highest successful run chase in Women's ODI history.

Not enough money: Adam Zampa dissatisfied with IPL pay, explains move to PSLAdam Zampa skipped the IPL 2026, citing dissatisfaction with the pay relative to his skills and the league's two-month schedule. Zampa is currently playing for Karachi Kings in PSL 2026.

Shreyas Iyer in trouble? Yuzvedra Chahal updates on Punjab skipper's injury scareIPL 2026, PBKS vs GT: Yuzvendra Chahal shared an update on Shreyas Iyer, who suffered an injury scare during Punjab Kings' clash against Gujarat Titans on Tuesday in Mullanpur.

Tiger Woods steps away from golf after DUI accident controversy, to seek helpTiger Woods has decided to step away from golf to focus on his health after pleading not guilty to DUI charges following a car crash in Florida. He has ruled himself out of the upcoming Augusta Masters to seek treatment.

Shreyas Iyer penalised in Punjab Kings' handsome IPL 2026 startIPL 2026, PBKS vs GT: Punjab Kings captain Shreyas Iyer has been fined Rs 12 lakh for a slow over-rate offence during PBKS' first match against Gujarat Titans. Iyer scored 18 off 11 as Punjab beat Gujarat by three wickets in Mullanpur.

Candidates: Hyper-aggressive Pragg suffers major setback, slips to 3rd spotCandidates Tournament: Indian Grand Master R Praggnanandhaa slipped to third place at the FIDE Candidates tournament after a loss to Javokhir Sindarov. Pragg faltered while trying to be aggressive against the 20-year-old Uzbekistani.

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".
